summary:
Ganglion cells within the optic nerve are predominantly damaged in glaucomatous optic neuropathy. This aligns with our understanding of how this condition progresses by selectively impairing these specific neurons crucial for vision transmission. Answer: C represents ganglion cell loss as a hallmark feature. 

The summary captures the essential point about which type of cellular structure is most significantly impacted while adhering closely to two sentence constraints provided initially. It also directly addresses what was asked without extraneous information or assumptions beyond those supported logically from typical knowledge bases regarding such conditions like glaucoma where detailed medical literature isn't available here but general principles can guide inference effectively based upon common educational materials used across healthcare professions training programs globally today.
